cards.front: What is the main difference between RMSprop and Adam?
cards.back: RMSprop uses the square root of the average of squared gradients, while Adam uses the exponential decay of the average of squared gradients and the first moment estimate.
RMSprop was introduced in 2014 by Geoffrey Hinton and his team.

cards.front: What is the name of the first moment estimate used in Adam?
cards.back: The first moment estimate used in Adam is the moving average of the gradient.

cards.front: What is the learning rate decay rate for Adam?
cards.back: The learning rate decay rate for Adam is typically set to 0.9.
